prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.4397) and high seasonality variance (0.3876), with ARIMA dominating trend tracking while ETS/THETA lead in seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveMovingAverage, ARIMA), indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance simultaneously, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths while minimizing individual model weaknesses.

    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4397,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,

  },

Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveMovingAverage, ARIMA), indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance simultaneously, DBA combination is the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths while minimizing individual model weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.104 shows moderate disagreement favoring DBA (recommended_method_hint=dba_combination) and weighted approaches. RMSE spread_ratio=0.4397 suggests top-k mean with k=5 balances diversity vs stability. ETS/THETA dominate early horizons while ARIMA leads late horizons, making best_per_horizon_by_validation valuable. shrinkage=0.35 handles the 26-model dispersion (tier1: ETS/THETA/ARIMA at RMSE~1000-1040 vs tier3 worst models >1300). Debate not forced as top candidates span distinct method families."
}
